metadata.description: Posterior cuff tendon providing external rotation strength.

metadata.description: Inferior posterior cuff muscle contributing to external rotation.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Lesion of the Talus Neighborhood
metadata.description: Superior labrum and biceps anchor complex at the glenoid origin.

metadata.claim_text: Osteochondral talar lesions cause persistent ankle pain after sprain and may need operative cartilage restoration.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Lesion of the Talus Neighborhood
metadata.claim_text: Osteochondral Lesion of the Talus pathoanatomy centers on talus, articular-cartilage and related stabilizers.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Lesion of the Talus Neighborhood
metadata.claim_text: Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating osteochondral lesion of the talus.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Lesion of the Talus Neighborhood
metadata.claim_text: Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for osteochondral lesion of the talus — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Lesion of the Talus Neighborhood
metadata.claim_text: State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for osteochondral lesion of the talus.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Lesion of the Talus Neighborhood
metadata.claim_text: Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for osteochondral lesion of the talus require attending-defined progression gates.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Lesion of the Talus Neighborhood
metadata.claim_text: Berndt and Harty classification describes talar osteochondral lesion stage and subchondral involvement.

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Lesion of the Talus Neighborhood
metadata.trigger: Active athlete with functional instability or mechanical symptoms from osteochondral lesion of the talus despite appropriate initial management
metadata.action: Discuss operative reconstruction versus continued rehabilitation; graft selection and fixation are attending decisions

metadata.review_packet_label: Osteochondral Lesion of the Talus Neighborhood
metadata.trigger: Athlete approaching return-to-sport after osteochondral lesion of the talus treatment
metadata.action: Apply sport-specific return-to-play criteria with strength, stability, and functional testing milestones
